text:
Ever tried applying a voltage multiplier on these potatoes? Hook up the potatoes in parallel to half down of the internal resistance of the potato to get higher voltage and current.
According to chatgpt, we'd need to have an oscillator there to have a current. Attach the potato in a spinning dc motor and jerk it back and forth between a copper coil? :D haha. I dunno. I'm just thinking out loud. I guess I need to buy some potatoes now.

author: @tenlittleindians
text:
Design a circuit board with traces as close together as possible without shorting. Arrange these to the edge of the printed circuit board with every other line connected together. You want two alternating rows so you can connect them up in series at two points on the board.

Leave half the traces in copper. The alternate traces would get zinc plated by the board maker.

Plug a series of these circuit boards into a potato and wire the cards together to make an even larger series array to maximize the output voltage from the potato.

Granted the current will be super low but you might find the voltage level very high!

author: @worldsgonemad4600
text:
Robert, I am the guy that has teased with the new graphene method, for about a year. I am going to give it away freely here right now and after I tell how I do it, I only ask that everyone read on as to why I have chose to not patent it and just let it ride freely. I call it the smear method. You need a piece of metal, I find those tin no trespassing signs work well. You need a fist sized piece of quartz crystal with at least one polished face. You need a nice puddle of elemental mercury metal I have found the best source is old plumb bobs are loaded with it, theres still ways to get it. Its reusable so, you just need about cup of it, forever. Also some high quality graphite, it doesn't have to be exfoliated, just use natural graphite dont try to use cheap lock grease, but if thats all you have and want to see this work go for it, your just going to wind up with contaminated mercury, and graphene. Ok heres whats up, for all you that haven't smacked your forehead yet and said, "Why didn't I think of this" by now. Take the sheet metal and fold the 4 sides so you have a square bowl with just high enough edges to keep the mercury contained, add mercury start small, dont worry about the metal amalgamating up it wont after doing this a year Ive only managed to get it to amalgam from using a drill bit and trying to force it to. Just pour it in, and sprinkle powdered graphite (I use a coffee bean grinder after crushing in a vice) on the mercury. The graphite sticks to the mercury, you will see it. Take the crystal and strike the mercury , what your doing is creating a piezoelectric effect with the metal which in turn causes an electromagnetic field in the metal, the mercury being diamagnetic and smashed tries to run away from this magnetic field, but due to the smearing action taking place it just breaks up into extremely, extremely tiny pieces.... :) One smear and repeat you dont need to slam it so hard it flies about, just a tap. When the face becomes marred run a raman on it. let the mercury either leach out or just distill it, I distill it just because graphene does weird stuff when its how like it condenses instead of expands. 
Ok the why part. The inventor of the x-ray machine gave me the tilting motivation, Ive been battling giving it away, or becoming uber rich for over a year. Here is my thinking, Ive never had money, lifes been a struggle since day one. My friends are all broke, and that is my life. Im happy though, I dont especially think money makes you better, but then a really good friend ran into a bunch of money and he turned into a complete asshole. No one likes him anymore, he thinks hes better than everyone else, and Ill be dammed if Im going to go out like that. I would rather die knowing I contributed to giving humanity what it needed to survive this current population issue we are facing. I think this very well can do just that, it will allow everyone to generate his or her own electricity. You can do that with graphene and its published. It will enable us to advance as a species and that is too valuable to horde it to myself, Im not really that smart I just watched a you tuber video about Graphene and had a dream in that dream I believe God gave me this to share and thats the only way I will ever remain sane knowing what I gave away today. Please enjoy and no one can claim this as hers or his, its ours.

author: @theinsider8872
text: Really cool thing I discovered with magnesium is how to make it work in a rechargeable battery.  I used Epsom salt which doesn't react with MG, and charged the battery using a carbon rode, then discharged using the magnesium and I was even able to leave the Mg in the battery.  This as you know is because MG doesn't like being recharged.  I used lead oxide for positive, and it was able to hold close to 3 volts!  Repeated charging and discharging worked really well.  The only problem I noticed is, the MG does start to slowly degrade, but you can still get a ton of cycles out of it! Keep these battery vids coming!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sorry mate to clarify are you saying Mg anode, Pb/PbO2 for cathode and epsom salt for electrolyte?

replies.author: @AgentBenzo
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering - likely :
Lead Ox impreg'd Carbon (Anode) ..... 
Magnesium as (Cathode) ... 
Epsom Salts as (Electrolyte).

replies.author: @theinsider8872
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering PBo2 is positive, MG is negative.  But you need a 2nd negative plate for charging, using carbon or lead works fine, just don't hook your charger to the magnesium.   I also tried graphite foil on its own as an positive and it worked really well too, in fact it's likely far more energy dense, just not as punchy as that tasty lead oxide.  :P  Just remember to have an additional cathode for recharging that is not MG!  I've only really tried 2 different other materials for this MG battery, lead and carbon.

replies.author: @theinsider8872
replies.text: Rik rik Yes I have thought about doing a video since I have a part time science channel.  Mg is crazy energetic.  So much voltage and amperage.

author: @rogerbraintree9552
text: Is there any way to create a potential between two or more salt bridges?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yes - but what advantage would you get from it?

replies.author: @rogerbraintree9552
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Well, I tried introducing a sodium chloride barrier between zinc sulphate and copper sulphate in the hope of increasing the voltage from 1 volt to 1.5 volts, but it made no difference.  Also the copper in the copper sulphate still showed up on the zinc electrode which I feel it shouldn't have done.  I was expecting to get sodium sulphate from the reaction and copper chloride.  Another battery I made was an aluminium sulphate battery in a pesto jar which pulled a current of 20 mA at 0.6 volts for several days using kitchen foil.  I made another which pulled nearly 500mA but only for a few minutes using more surface area.  I gave up on aluminium foil because it was too fiddly to prepare all the strips.  But I was wondering if powdered aluminium mixed with carbon soaked in aluminium sulphate might be made to pull a high current.  An easy way to make a zinc sulphate cell is to soak cotton wool in zinc sulphate, sprinkle a few crystals of copper sulphate on the copper plate before hand.  The advantage being that it makes it less spillable and slows the copper sulphate from over mixing into the zinc sulphate, and that it can easily be disassembled after use.
